With all due respect to everyone, can we please stop saying things like "great news" and "go south/go north"? I understand that no one wants it to hit them directly, but saying things like this just puts unnecessary stress and anxiety on those north or south of you. None of us need or deserve this any more than anyone else. It's no one's "turn" to take the direct hit. Our entire coastline is already hurting right now, and the majority of us are already suffering PTSD from one storm or another.
Be safe, be smart, but also be kind.

I’ve always been able to get out of the way for hurricanes, but I’m staying with friends in a second floor condo near Orlando. Any specific prep we should make sure we do today aside from having food stocked for power outage?
I would make sure your car is in a place safe from flooding. My sister in law was safe from Helene on the 2nd floor, but her car got flooded. Stay safe
freeze water, so when the power goes out you can place it in the fridge and keep some in your freezer. May help save some of your food. Prayers for your family.
I evacuated from the TBA and am here in Orlando at a hotel. We’ll get knocked around a bit with wind and rain so expect power outages and potential broken windows if it’s a cat 2 with projectiles.
just be prepared for a long power outage
If your windows are not hurricane strength it would be best to cover them.

make sure you have water. You won’t be able to flush toilets if you lose power. We have 2 liter bottles filled with water specifically for flushing. You can also fill your bathtub for flushing. Don’t forget water to drink. Also flashlights.

For a Cat 2, you want to cover your windows. There will be a lot of flying debris. I've sat through Cat 2 winds before. It removed the shingles from many roofs and damaged weaker structures such as mobile homes.
